description_long: This is a commercial passenger airplane with a streamlined fuselage, swept-back wings, and a classic silver livery featuring a red, white, and blue stripe along the body. The aircraft displays the American Airlines logo on the vertical stabilizer and has two large turbofan engines mounted under the wings. The wings are low-mounted and slightly swept, with no visible winglets. The tail section features a conventional design with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers. The cockpit is located at the forward end of the fuselage, with multiple windows visible. The airplane's surface appears metallic and smooth, with a glossy finish reflecting light.
description: A silver commercial passenger airplane with swept-back low wings, underwing turbofan engines, and American Airlines livery. It features a streamlined fuselage, a conventional tail, and a glossy metallic surface.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the fuselage, American Airlines logo on the tail, and both engines under the wings.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlighting the wingspan, engine placement, and the T-shaped layout of the tail and wings.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the other profile of the fuselage, tail logo, and underwing engines.
description_view_3: Frontal view showing the nose, cockpit windows, engine intakes, and the leading edges of the wings.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing configuration with moderately swept-back wings and no visible winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers
attribute_landing_gear_type: Retractable tricycle landing gear, with main and nose gear units implied but not visible
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed multi-seat cockpit with multiple windows
